commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - Protected maintainer PR with compatibility and secret-workflow product decisions; automation should not pick a permanent smoke-ci contract on its own.; Needs attention: The dependency graph concern from earlier heads is cleared, but the new secret-bearing Convex/Telegram workflow lane still needs explicit maintainer/security approval.; Review finding: [P1] Keep smoke-ci deterministic until Crabline supports the fu... (sha=806a505a02a4f99e27d70f1935986ac2045a9ab8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Crabline-backed QA Lab channel driver and Telegram transport, exposes profile/CLI scenario/channel-driver selection, updates smoke/release profile defaults and docs, and adds a Convex-backed smoke-ci workflow lane.

PR surface: Source +1384, Tests +745, Docs +12, Config +93, Other +2. Total +2236 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main blocker by source inspection: smoke-ci selects scenarios that call handleQaAction, while the PR's Crabline transport throws for generic message actions. I did not run the live lane because this review is read-only and the needed Telegram credentials are secret-brokered.
commands.review_followup: - Protected maintainer PR with compatibility and secret-workflow product decisions; automation should not pick a permanent smoke-ci contract on its own.

target.title: feat(qa): crabline channel driver
target.body:
## Summary

- Wires profile-selected `channelDriver: crabline` into QA suite execution instead of only writing Crabline metadata artifacts.
- Adds a Crabline-backed Telegram QA transport that sends scenario inbound text through Crabline, configures a real OpenClaw Telegram gateway account for SUT replies, and mirrors observed replies back into the existing QA Lab bus.
- Reuses the existing QA credential lease path so Crabline Telegram can read direct `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_*` env locally or lease Telegram credentials from Convex in CI with `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=convex`.
- Adds `qa run --qa-profile ... --scenario ...` filtering so CI can run a small taxonomy/profile-owned smoke slice while still using the profile's `channelDriver`.
- Adds a secret-bearing workflow lane that runs the `smoke-ci` profile through Crabline with Convex credential leases.
- Pins QA Lab's dev-only Crabline dependency to merged `openclaw/crabline@ee1189ad966e9e8d7eea9e38b3ca5965ff48d93c` and lazy-loads it so production OpenClaw dist does not bundle Crabline provider code.

## Linked context

- Uses merged openclaw/crabline#9, which exports the runtime registry/provider API from Crabline's package root.
- Builds on the earlier profile/taxonomy work in this PR: `smoke-ci` selects `crabline`, `release` declares `live`, and default profiles continue to use `qa-channel`.
- Related follow-up openclaw/openclaw#91506 preserves suite selections across the isolated runner wrapper.

## CI credential setup

- CI does not need raw `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_GROUP_ID`, `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN`, or `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_SUT_BOT_TOKEN` secrets for this lane.
- The new `run_smoke_ci_crabline` job in `qa-live-transports-convex.yml` uses the existing `qa-live-shared` secrets: `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SITE_URL` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CONVEX_SECRET_CI`.
- The job sets `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=convex` and `OPENCLAW_QA_CREDENTIAL_ROLE=ci`, then runs `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --provider-mode mock-openai --fast`.

## Real behavior proof

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts` passes 141/141 tests, including coverage for importing the installed Crabline package and acquiring Telegram credentials through the Convex broker path.
- `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/crabline-no-env-check --provider-mode mock-openai --fast` resolves `smoke-ci` to one scenario and fails locally at the direct-env fallback with `OPENCLAW_QA_TELEGRAM_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN is required for --channel-driver crabline telegram.` That proves the taxonomy profile enters Crabline; CI satisfies this with the Convex credential source.
- A full live Telegram smoke run was not possible in this shell because the Telegram credentials are intentionally brokered through CI secrets.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/qa-lab/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm openclaw qa run --qa-profile smoke-ci --category channel-framework.conversation-routing-and-delivery --scenario dm-chat-baseline --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/crabline-no-env-check --provider-mode mock-openai --fast` expectedly fails locally at missing direct Telegram QA env.
- `git diff --check`

## Production bundle/dependency check

- `crabline` appears only in `extensions/qa-lab` `devDependencies`, not `dependencies` or `peerDependencies`.
- Previous clean build verification on this branch showed `rg "node_modules/crabline|providers/builtin" dist -n` returns no matches.
- Built `dist` still contains expected QA strings like `channel-driver crabline`, but not Crabline provider/runtime chunks.

## Current limits

- QA Lab's Crabline execution transport currently supports Telegram only. Non-Telegram Crabline selections fail explicitly.
- Generic message actions like reaction/edit/delete are not implemented through the Crabline Telegram transport yet.
- Telegram direct QA conversations are represented through the configured Telegram QA group because Telegram bots cannot DM each other.
